summary: Soundboard recording plagued by extreme pitch‑speed distortion, yet the performance shines with an energetic Half Step jam and a standout Peggy‑O.
review: The show is marked by high energy – Garcia’s excited solo on the Half Step and a memorable Peggy‑O earn praise – but the source suffers from serious technical flaws. Reviewers report a splice glitch, overly fast playback and obvious pitch correction that make the tape sound like it was run at ~20 RPM, relegating the recording quality to poor despite decent musicianship.

ai_rating.rationale: Performance notes are generally positive (energy, standout songs) while technical issues dominate the recording feedback, leading to a middling overall rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Splice glitch in the middle of the jam, extreme speed/pitch distortion (appears ~20 RPM fast), and noticeable pitch‑correction artifacts; overall clarity is compromised.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The set flows with high‑energy jams, highlighted by an enthusiastic Half Step segment and a strong Peggy‑O; the ending of Franklin’s Tower receives special mention for its unique jam.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia is described as thrilled during his second solo on the Half Step, though vocal pitch sounds unnatural due to recording speed issues.
band_member_comments.Bob: Weir unleashes great chords toward the end of Franklin’s Tower, adding a distinctive jam element.
